cards.title: Dogs in Public Places: Where Should the Line Be?
cards.body: More dogs are appearing in restaurants, offices, and supermarkets, with some even petitioning for flight access. Not everyone welcomes this trend, especially parents with young children who may feel uncomfortable.

cards.title: Raising Emotionally Healthy Children
cards.body: Psychologist Lindsay C Gibson's book on emotionally immature parents became a pandemic bestseller. Her new advice focuses on raising happy, healthy children by breaking cycles of emotional immaturity.

cards.title: The Challenge of Parenting Teenagers
cards.body: Parents of teenagers doing A-levels face unique helplessness watching their children navigate intense academic pressure. The emotional toll on parents can be as stressful as it is for students.

cards.title: Paternal Postnatal Depression Often Goes Undiagnosed
cards.body: New fathers experience depression at significant rates, yet it's frequently overlooked by healthcare providers. Recognizing and treating paternal postpartum depression is crucial for family wellbeing.

cards.title: Trauma-Informed Parenting: Breaking Protective Cycles
cards.body: Parents who grew up in unsafe environments may become hypervigilant, overprotecting their own children. Understanding how trauma shapes parenting can help break unhealthy patterns.

cards.title: Family Space and Healthy Child Development
cards.body: Living apart from family members can bring guilt, but healthy distance supports growth in children, parents, and partners. Love and connection can thrive even with physical separation.

cards.title: Fatherhood, Forgiveness, and Generational Legacy
cards.body: Fathers must embrace their imperfections rather than hide them from adult children. This authenticity creates a more loving intergenerational legacy.

cards.title: Understanding Pathological Demand Avoidance in Children
cards.body: Pathological demand avoidance (PDA) involves protective mechanisms that activate before conscious choice. Understanding this pattern helps parents support children more effectively.

cards.title: VAT Cuts on Family Activities and Meals
cards.body: The UK government has cut VAT on children's meals and theme park tickets to ease cost-of-living pressures. These savings aim to make family outings more affordable.

cards.title: Child Safety: Biological Sex in Facilities
cards.body: UK guidance confirms toilets and changing rooms must be used based on biological sex. This policy impacts how schools and public spaces manage child safety.
